user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.

Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs. Calls to these services would have experienced higher latency or elevated errors in the form of HTTP 502 response codes. Increased error rate.

Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency. Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4. Customers might face connectivity issues.

Symptoms: Increased error rate. Google Front End (GFE) prevented API requests from reaching the service. CreateBuild requests that did make it to the servers were more likely to fail. Cloud Firestore saw 80% of listen streams become disconnected and a 50% drop in query/get requests. Requests to compute.googleapis.com would have seen an increase in 502 errors. Cloud Spanner experienced elevated latency spikes which may have resulted in connection timeouts. Minor connectivity problems. Unavailable for viewing in the Cloud Console.
Affected customers may see errors when trying to query their monitoring data. Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data.

We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Google Cloud infrastructure components, the issue manifests itself as periods of increased latency every 30 minutes. Cloud Networking traffic going through us-central1 and us-east1 shows increased latency.

APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may ended up as a loading failure. Several Cloud console pages experiencing slow loading and loading errors.
We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL
Google Compute Engine customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss in southamerica-east1-[a, b, c]
Degraded performance for PD-Standard devices. Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b.

Customers were unable to load the console or experienced timeouts, unable to connect to services, experienced unavailable errors and latency, services were unavailable, experienced issues receiving notifications, experienced probe creation request failures, unable to create triggers, and experienced elevated errors.

service_name: Multiple Products (Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform)
user_symptom: A small number of users of the following Google Workspace products experienced HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors. Affected customers experienced elevated API traffic error rates. Customers may have experienced generic unavailable errors. Customers may have experienced elevated latency, operation deadline expirations.
Significant packet loss on Cloud Interconnect in europe-west2 for some customers. Visible, near 100% packet loss on Cloud Interconnect.
Queries exhibited higher than normal latency and delays in query execution

Affected customers may have experienced delayed log entries in Log Explorer or other export destinations. Log entries written in europe-west3 are delayed by 1+ hours. They may not show up in Log Explorer or other export destinations.

Google Cloud customers using VMWare Engine experienced connectivity issues while using vSphere, vCenter and NSX. Affected Customers may have observed issues with network partitions and accessing workload VMs. Affected Customers may have experienced connectivity issues using vCenter, HCX, NSX Manager, and NSX-T Edge virtual machines.
Cloud Logging logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Cloud logs might be unavailable for 25% of customers in global location. Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Issues with seeing monitoring metric data especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.
Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer

new connections to Cloud Pub/Sub experienced high latency leading to timeouts and elevated error rates; Cloud Pub/Sub publish, pull and ack methods experienced elevated latency, leading to timeouts; error rates peaked at 4.1% for publish, 5.7% for ack
HTTP load balancing returning error for some user in Europe; increased TLS handshake errors or connection terminations for HTTPS traffic served from load balancers in Western Europe
Users experiencing failure with file uploads. The case attachment upload feature has been disabled for all Cloud Console users.
Customers may not be able to look at live logs by running 'tail' commands

Customers using Google Cloud Networking experienced a network traffic disruption. Some customers using Cloud Networking experienced severe traffic disruption. Some cloud customers communicating outside the affected regions (including to the Internet) would have seen two periods of disruption.
Containers within GKE cluster node pools using docker are getting restarted in the event of docker restarts. GKE cluster pods restart when docker restarts.

Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail, customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d
preventing customers from creating or updating Support cases. Cloud Console pages that are nested under https://console.cloud.google.com/support may have also failed to load or update.
Attempts to create a new instance or to delete an instance fail with an error
This may appear as timeouts, 5XX errors and potential authorization errors being returned from the service.
Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted. Clusters may see pods restarting when Dockers are restarted.

Customers impacted by this issue would have experienced abnormal instance terminations for instances which were running in the impacted building. Customers were unable to create PD devices in the impacted zone. Some customers experiencing service unavailability for impacted products. Some customers with data replicated only in the impacted region experienced HTTP 500 errors. Customers may have experienced high error rates. Some customers experienced timeouts and degraded service. Some customers experienced elevated latency.

Cloud SQL External Server or Database Migration Service (DMS) users who migrate to Cloud SQL for MySQL 5.7 with GTID replication using managed dumps may experience errors if they have insert/update/delete transactions during the dump phase. Furthermore, if updates were made to those jobs during the replication phase, the migration job may fail.

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased latency, packet loss, and service unavailable errors for traffic between regions and from Google to external endpoints including on-premises environments and the internet.
connectivity loss affecting Cloud Networking in us-central1 resulting in failures and elevated latency
users attempting to update or deploy Cloud Load Balancers may experience delays or failures
Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer

customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. It is possible for customers to experience service interruption in none, one, or both zones. Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail.
Customers are unable to login to their admin workstation in both their prod and non-prod environments
Customers will see a 'Failed to load' error with following message: 'There was an error while loading /cloud-build/triggers?project= Please try again'

Some customers may be experiencing connection failures in Docker workflow to GCLB, GCB, or Fastly destinations such as debian.org, github.com with a timeout error

Google BigQuery experienced elevated query latencies and job failures globally. Affected customers would have experienced elevated query latencies or in some cases query failures.

customers were unable to create or edit Google Compute Engine (GCE) instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error 'Constraint constraints/compute.vmExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].'

Some users are experiencing connectivity issues between VMs or with onprem. Customers were unable to access their Cloud Volume Service (CVS) storage across GCP Regions. Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) experienced connectivity issues between instances and/or with on-premises machines.

config changes will be accepted & fail to propagate; Cloud Armor rules may also be affected; Appengine flex customers may see apps fail to deploy; Traffic Director may see configs fail to propagate; VM instance group healthchecks & functionality like autoscaling/autohealing may be affected

Customer may experience replication failure on the new CSA rollout on replica, if the master instance is busy with INSERTs/UPDATEs on tables with timestamp column and session timezone is set to other than SYSTEM.
Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors. Cloud Run deployments timing out and failing.

Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation. Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories and would have received failure messages. Affected customers were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors. Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets and would have been unable to create new clusters.

Google Cloud App Engine customers might have experienced elevated errors while using the Secrets Manager. Customers might have experienced increased errors from apps or functions that use the Secrets Manager.
Cloud Composer v2 environments autoscaling might not work. Number of Airflow workers in the Composer environment won't change.

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased packet loss for egress traffic from Google to the Middle East, and elevated latency between our Europe and Asia Regions. Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts.

Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed. Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes. Customer are unable to make cloud armour config changes globally.

Cloud Function 2nd Generation deletes are erroring out in multiple regions. Delete function operations are erroring with following message: Operation Failed. Public Error Status: code: 5 message: Delete package operation for projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[REGION]/repositories/gcf-artifacts/packages/[FUNCTION_ID]%2Fcustom-run has failed. Additional information: Requested entity was not found.
Cloud SQL for SQL Server is seeing a spike in instance creation failures globally. Increased failure rate for SQLServer instance creation.
Customers experienced a cloud networking disruption from 04:30 AM - 04:58 AM US/Pacific

Customers would have received a failure message of 'Request contains an invalid argument.' Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors. Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets. Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters. Customers will be getting IAM_BACKEND_INVALID_ARGUMENT. Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation.
